title: The Iron in Your Blood Came From a Dying Star — The Qur'an Said It Was 'Sent Down'
_note: CINEMATIC, hook-first. Footage = molten/abstract/galaxy/meteor/earth (no humans; thumbnail-verified; foundry clips avoided due to worker risk). NO recital. Simple English. SOURCE: Surah Al-Hadid 57:25 — 'and We sent down iron, in it is great might and benefits for people.' SCIENCE (solid): iron forms by fusion in massive stars and is scattered by supernovae (stellar nucleosynthesis); Earth's iron is of cosmic origin; the radioactive isotope iron-60 found on Earth and the Moon proves extraterrestrial delivery. Honest 'sent down' framing. Safe-zone captions. #HistoryTikTokContest.

segments.text: The iron in your blood, in your phone, in this whole planet, did not come from Earth. And the Quran hinted at this fourteen hundred years ago.
The Quran says iron was, quote, sent down.
For centuries, that was a strange way to describe a metal we dig out of the ground.
Then science found where iron really comes from. It cannot form on Earth. It is forged inside giant, dying stars.
When those stars explode, they fling iron across space. And billions of years ago, some of it fell to Earth.
Every piece of iron you have ever touched was once inside a star, and was sent down from the sky.
And We sent down iron, in which is great strength, and benefits for the people.
Sent down. Exactly as the Quran said.

sources: Qur'an, Surah Al-Hadid 57:25 — 'and We sent down iron, wherein is great might and benefits for the people.'

Science: iron is produced by fusion in massive stars and dispersed by supernovae (stellar nucleosynthesis); it cannot be created on Earth. Earth's iron is of cosmic origin. The radioactive isotope iron-60 found on Earth and the Moon confirms extraterrestrial delivery (e.g., IFLScience; ESA).
Qur'an 57:25 shown as Arabic + English; not recited.
VISUALS: all Pexels clips verified by thumbnail — no human faces/bodies (industrial foundry clips were deliberately avoided to prevent any worker appearing).
